warps.notes: Main exit — returns to Route 32 exterior (x11,y73)
Main exit (second tile) — returns to Route 32 exterior
Stairs to Pokecenter 2F (Cable Club / Link area)

tile_layout.notes: Standard Pokemon Center interior layout. Nurse at counter (x3,y1), Fishing Guru at x1,y4, Cool Trainer F at x6,y2. Stairs to 2F at x0,y6. Three exit tiles at the south wall.

npcs.name: Nurse Joy
npcs.dialogue_summary: Standard Pokemon Center nurse. Heals all party Pokemon for free.

npcs.name: Fishing Guru
npcs.dialogue_summary: Asks the player if they want to try fishing. If yes, gives the Old Rod for free. After receiving it, he encourages the player to fish wherever there is water.
npcs.notes: This is the first and only freely-given rod in the early game. Key item for accessing Water-type Pokemon from Route 32 onward.

npcs.name: Cool Trainer (female)
npcs.dialogue_summary: Wonders what held item to give her Pokemon — maybe something that raises Attack power.

items.name: Old Rod
items.notes: Given by the Fishing Guru. Allows fishing for Magikarp and Tentacool at Old Rod spots.

events.description: Elm's aide waits here to give the player the Togepi Egg (Miracle Egg). This is triggered by Cool Trainer M's dialogue outside on Route 32 pointing the player here.
events.notes: The aide and the Egg handoff happen elsewhere in the script chain (flagged as EVENT_GOT_TOGEPI_EGG_FROM_ELMS_AIDE) but the Pokemon Center is the location referenced by Cool Trainer M.

notes: Mid-route Pokemon Center located at approximately y73 on Route 32. This is one of only two route-based Pokemon Centers in Johto (the other is on Route 40). Key purpose: healing point partway through the long Route 32 traverse. Also where the Fishing Guru gives out the Old Rod. Pokecrystal source: maps/Route32Pokecenter1F.asm. Exact interior dimensions not specified in pokecrystal; using standard Pokecenter footprint (5x4 blocks / 10x8 tiles).
